steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1 states that Rogerson’s calculation was more accurate than the 1920 one. Sentence 2 explains why that difference is unsurprising: the 1920 method used only cardboard and string. The blank must signal that what follows is a natural, expected consequence of what came before — not a purpose, a similarity, or a final conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To that end,’: signals that Sentence 2 is a purposeful action taken to achieve the goal stated in Sentence 1 — but Sentence 2 explains a historical fact, not a purposeful step. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Of course,’: signals that what follows is unsurprising given the context — rudimentary cardboard-and-string equipment naturally yields less accurate results. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Likewise,’: signals a parallel situation — Sentence 2 explains a reason, not a parallel case. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Ultimately,’: signals a culminating conclusion — Sentence 2 is an explanatory aside, not a final result. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
